info.title: API Documentation

get.description: Retrieve a list of cloud account objects.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Unique object identifier, either a UUID primary key or a composite key.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Number of results to return per page.

parameters.name: offset
parameters.description: The initial index from which to return the results.

parameters.name: sort
parameters.description: Which field to use when ordering the results.

parameters.name: exclude_m2m
parameters.description: Exclude many-to-many fields from the response

get.description: Retrieve a prefix object.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: A UUID string identifying this prefix.

parameters.name: exclude_m2m
parameters.description: Exclude many-to-many fields from the response

get.description:
A convenience method for listing and/or allocating available IP addresses within a prefix.

By default, the number of IPs returned will be equivalent to PAGINATE_COUNT.
An arbitrary limit (up to MAX_PAGE_SIZE, if set) may be passed, however results will not be paginated.

This uses a Redis lock to prevent this API from being invoked in parallel, in order to avoid a race condition
if multiple clients tried to simultaneously request allocation from the same parent prefix.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: A UUID string identifying this IP address.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Number of results to return per page.

parameters.name: offset
parameters.description: The initial index from which to return the results.

parameters.name: range_end
parameters.description: IP from which enumeration/allocation should stop.

parameters.name: range_start
parameters.description: IP from which enumeration/allocation should start.

parameters.name: sort
parameters.description: Which field to use when ordering the results.

parameters.name: exclude_m2m
parameters.description: Exclude many-to-many fields from the response

get.description:
A convenience method for listing and/or allocating available child prefixes within a parent.

This uses a Redis lock to prevent this API from being invoked in parallel, in order to avoid a race condition
if multiple clients tried to simultaneously request allocation from the same parent prefix.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: A UUID string identifying this prefix.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Number of results to return per page.

parameters.name: offset
parameters.description: The initial index from which to return the results.

parameters.name: sort
parameters.description: Which field to use when ordering the results.

parameters.name: exclude_m2m
parameters.description: Exclude many-to-many fields from the response

Note.description:
This base serializer implements common fields and logic for all ModelSerializers.

Namely, it:

- defines the `display` field which exposes a human friendly value for the given object.
- ensures that `id` field is always present on the serializer as well.
- ensures that `created` and `last_updated` fields are always present if applicable to this model and serializer.
- ensures that `object_type` field is always present on the serializer which represents the content-type of this
  serializer's associated model (e.g. "dcim.device"). This is required as the OpenAPI schema, using the
  PolymorphicProxySerializer class defined below, relies upon this field as a way to identify to the client
  which of several possible serializers are in use for a given attribute.
- supports `?depth` query parameter. It is passed in as `nested_depth` to the `build_nested_field()` function
  to enable the dynamic generation of nested serializers.
